canonical_question: What are the Salesforce Apex governor limits per transaction - SOQL, DML, CPU, heap, callouts?
aliases: Salesforce Apex governor limits complete list sync async
SOQL query limit per transaction Salesforce
Salesforce DML statement limit per transaction
Salesforce CPU time limit heap size callout limits

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Spring '26 (v66.0) — Apex Cursors GA for large SOQL result sets

constraints: Governor limits are per-transaction — all triggers/flows/automations share ONE pool
Cascading triggers consume from the SAME transaction pool
Limits cannot be increased by Salesforce support — hard platform limits
Callout-before-DML rule: cannot make callouts after uncommitted DML
Mixed DML restriction: cannot mix setup and non-setup object DML
Sandbox orgs have LOWER daily API limits than production

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need daily/rolling API call limits (not per-transaction)
Need Bulk API 2.0 limits and best practices
Need Platform Event delivery and publishing allocations

related_to.label: Salesforce REST API Capabilities & Rate Limits

often_confused_with.label: Daily API call limits (org-level) vs per-transaction governor limits

inputs_needed.question: Is your code running synchronously or asynchronously?
options: synchronous (trigger, Visualforce, REST/SOAP API, Lightning, Flow)
asynchronous (@future, Queueable, Batch Apex, Scheduled Apex)

inputs_needed.question: Which governor limit are you hitting or worried about?
Does your transaction involve cascading triggers across multiple objects?
